description_long: This object is a video game controller with a distinctive trident shape, featuring three hand grips and a central joystick. The front face displays a classic D-pad on the left, a large red button in the center, and a cluster of four colored buttons (yellow and blue) on the right, along with a gray analog stick below the central button. The back reveals a slot for an accessory or memory card, and the underside shows three trigger buttons, one on each grip. The controller is primarily gray with black accents and colored buttons, and its ergonomic design is intended for handheld use. The surface appears to be made of hard plastic, typical for consumer electronics. This type of controller is commonly associated with the Nintendo 64 gaming console.
description: A gray, trident-shaped video game controller with colored buttons, a central joystick, and ergonomic hand grips. It features a classic design with a mix of buttons and an accessory slot on the back.
description_view_0: Front view showing the D-pad, central red button, analog stick, and colored action buttons.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the controller and the protruding analog stick.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the accessory slot and the three trigger buttons on the grips.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the profile of the controller and the button layout.
attribute_control_interface: physical buttons, analog stick, D-pad, trigger buttons
